title: Smart Cities
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Smart cities use technology and data to improve the quality (0) of life for residents while reducing environmental impact. As urban populations continue to grow, cities are increasingly turning to smart technologies to manage resources more efficiently.||Smart city technologies include everything (9) from intelligent traffic management systems to sensor networks that monitor air quality. These systems collect vast amounts of data, (10) which is then analysed to help city planners make better decisions. For example, smart traffic lights can adjust their timing based (11) on real-time traffic flow, reducing congestion and emissions.||Many cities have installed smart energy grids (12) that can balance electricity supply and demand more effectively. Smart waste management systems use sensors in bins to optimise collection routes, while smart water systems can detect leaks and reduce waste. Public transport (13) has also benefited from smart technology, with real-time tracking apps helping passengers plan their journeys more efficiently.||However, smart cities face challenges. The technology requires significant investment, and there (14) are concerns about data privacy and security. Critics worry that not all residents are able (15) to benefit equally from these innovations, potentially creating a digital divide. Despite these issues, the smart city concept continues to evolve, (16) with more cities worldwide adopting these technologies to create more sustainable and liveable urban environments.

example.text: Smart cities use technology and data to improve the quality (0) life for residents.

questions.explanation: Expression 'everything from X to Y' indicates a full range of technologies.
Relative pronoun referring to the collected data.
Expression 'based on' means founded on something.
Relative pronoun referring to smart electrical grids.
Present perfect: 'has benefited' indicates a benefit up to the present.
Simple present: 'there are concerns' with a plural subject.
The infinitive marker 'to' is required after the adjective 'able' to form the construction 'able to + infinitive'.
Preposition 'with' introduces additional information about cities.
